# Report Rubric (100 points)

## 1) Technical Completeness (30)
- 0-10: Architecture and module boundaries are clear
- 0-10: Scheduling/timing model is explained with concrete periods
- 0-10: Filtering/calibration/fault logic is explained with formulas or thresholds

## 2) Evidence and Verifiability (30)
- 0-10: Build verification includes command, outcome, and artifacts
- 0-10: Runtime evidence includes key serial logs or explicit unverified label
- 0-10: Claim-to-source mapping exists and covers critical claims

## 3) Reproducibility (20)
- 0-10: Build/deploy steps are executable and environment assumptions are explicit
- 0-10: Troubleshooting steps are actionable and testable

## 4) Clarity and Report Quality (20)
- 0-5: First-mentioned technical terms are explained in plain language
- 0-5: Quick Start path is executable on first read
- 0-5: Core-feature functions include clear input/output/side-effect explanations
- 0-5: Narrative flow, checkpoints, and terminology consistency

## Pass Threshold
- Recommended pass threshold: >= 85
- If score < 85, revise weakest category first, then re-score.
